assertions.expectation: Explains that spot VMs can be preempted at any time, which interacts with the upgrade drain process
assertions.reason: Response mentions spot interruptions and preemptions throughout, showing awareness of how they interact with upgrades

assertions.expectation: Notes that spot node pools may already have workloads designed for interruption (good for upgrades)
assertions.reason: Response discusses workloads that 'can handle spot interruptions' and shows taints/tolerations for spot-tolerant workloads

assertions.expectation: Recommends higher maxSurge for spot pools since the workloads should already be fault-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response shows maxSurge=1 and maxUnavailable=0, which is conservative rather than higher surge for fault-tolerant workloads

assertions.expectation: Advises setting maxUnavailable >0 for spot pools since workloads are already preemption-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response sets maxUnavailable=0, contradicting the expectation to use >0 for preemption-tolerant workloads

assertions.expectation: Mentions that spot VM availability may affect surge node creation (not guaranteed to get spot instances)
assertions.reason: Response includes checking spot instance availability and acknowledges availability concerns

assertions.expectation: Recommends having PDBs even for spot workloads to ensure orderly drain during upgrade
assertions.reason: Response includes PodDisruptionBudget configuration and emphasizes reviewing disruption budgets

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command for configuring surge settings on the spot pool
assertions.reason: Response provides the exact gcloud command with max-surge-upgrade and max-unavailable-upgrade parameters

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading spot pools before on-demand pools since they're lower risk
assertions.reason: Response suggests upgrading on-demand nodes first, then spot pools, which is opposite to the expectation
